DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS WILLIAM ROBINSON, WILLIAM G ROBINSON, ACCOUNTANCY CORPORATION, PAMELA RAMIREZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendants Pamela Ramirez, William G. Robinson, and William G. Robinson Accountancy Corporation's demurrer to all causes of actions in the first amended complaint filed is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to all causes of action. As to the claims against Ms. Ramirez, plaintiffs are given leave to amend to allege facts showing that the one-year statute of limitations per CCP 366.2(a) was tolled because they timely filed a creditor's claims and the executor failed to allow, approve, or reject the claims. When a cause of action appears to be barred by an applicable statute of limitations, plaintiffs must plead facts which show tolling to avoid the statutory bar. (Ponderosa Homes, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 1761, 1768.) The portions of the claims seeking damages based on Laura and Greg Torlai's tax years 2005, 2006, and 2007 and Stony Creek Land & Cattle Company, Inc.'s tax years ending March 2006 and March 2007 are time-barred by the two-year statute of limitations in CCP 339(1). Receipt of notices of deficiency more than two years prior to the filing of the complaint bars allegations of accounting malpractice, however labeled, for the years for which the notices were received. (Curtis v. Kellogg & Andelson (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 492, 500-501.) As to all other tax years for which plaintiffs seek damages, plaintiffs are given leave to allege precisely which tax years they are seeking damages and to allege that they did not receive a notice of deficiency for those tax years more than two years before this case was filed.

Counsel for defendants is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.31312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org.
